authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Fund Transfers (Regulation E)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal consumer-protection rule for electronic fund transfers, error resolution, disclosures, and records; authoritative for payment workflow controls and customer-impact boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Remittance Transfers Small Entity Compliance Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB guide for remittance-transfer disclosures, receipts, cancellations, and error handling, authoritative for cross-border payment workflow stages.

authoritative_sources.title: Truth in Lending (Regulation Z)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal consumer-credit rule for billing-error and periodic-statement workflows, authoritative for charge, fee, invoice, and dispute analytics boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Operating Circular 4
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Federal Reserve ACH operating rule establishing settlement, return, and processing expectations that govern payment and posting workflow semantics.

authoritative_sources.title: Filing Information for Suspicious Activity Reports
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN SAR filing guidance is authoritative for suspicious-activity escalation and reporting boundaries when anomalies appear in financial analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Exception escalation for suspicious activity, customer harm risk, or policy interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML InfoBase
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized supervisory control framework covering suspicious activity, customer risk, and governance expectations; authoritative for control-based escalation posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Exception escalation for suspicious activity, customer harm risk, or policy interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: A Framework for OFAC Compliance Commitments
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of Foreign Assets Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary OFAC compliance framework used to determine when blocked parties, sanctions-related controls, or override attempts require escalation rather than analytical synthesis.

authoritative_sources.title: Unfair Claims Settlement Practices Act
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public insurance model-law baseline describing claim investigation, communication, and settlement workflow expectations; authoritative as a baseline model while state adoption still matters.
used_for: unresolved ambiguity handling for state variation

authoritative_sources.title: Bank-to-Customer Cash Management
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized payment and cash-management messaging standard defining statements and reconciliation semantics across payment and account workflows.

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect account, policy, transaction, posting, claim, dispute, servicing, disclosure, and reconciliation records and confirm the authoritative truth set before analysis begins.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Metric calculation or report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Define metric logic, denominators, event-date rules, source hierarchy, refresh assumptions, and audience-specific reporting packages without collapsing payment or claim states.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis or disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Translate calculated outputs into analytical narratives or disclosure-adjacent language while preserving caveats, unresolved reconciliation, and consumer-impact boundaries.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data
workflow_stages.description: Stop or downgrade outputs when source extracts are stale, denominator logic is disputed, documentation is missing, or systems disagree on the financial outcome.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Exception escalation for suspicious activity, customer harm risk, or policy interpretation
workflow_stages.description: Route fraud, AML, sanctions, consumer-impact, legal-interpretation, claims, or accounting-authority cases to adjacent specialists with evidence-backed anomaly summaries.

artifacts: account statements, transaction histories, payment records, and remittance details
policy records, premium records, claim files, denial or adjudication notes, and servicing logs
invoices, fee schedules, dispute files, chargeback records, refund records, and write-off requests
general-ledger, sub-ledger, settlement, suspense, reconciliation, and adjustment reports
KYC, AML, sanctions, fraud, complaint, escalation, and approval-history artifacts
customer communications, disclosures, notices, call logs, and servicing correspondence
systems_of_record: core banking or policy administration systems

allowed: Define metrics, source hierarchy, refresh assumptions, and caveat structure for financial-services analytics requests.
Produce evidence-backed analytical briefs, posting summaries, reconciliation notes, and data-quality or disclosure-risk escalation outputs.
Normalize financial-services terminology and preserve source lineage without implying unsupported authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, source precedence, jurisdiction, or product scope materially change the metric outcome.
Fraud, AML, sanctions, consumer-impact, legal-interpretation, claim-adjudication, or accounting-treatment issues change the safe reporting posture.
Different systems produce unreconciled outcomes for the same reporting period or disclosure context.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, claim adjudication, underwriting approval, accounting treatment, fraud closure, or final disclosure approval.
Requests for stronger causal, trend, benchmark, or override claims than the records support.
Publication requests where records are incomplete, stale, or conflicting enough to change the conclusion.

domain_failure_modes: Using a financial metric denominator that mixes incompatible workflow states such as pending, posted, reversed, settled, denied, or adjudicated items.
Treating stale payment, servicing, or ledger extracts as current and publishing unsupported trend conclusions.
Collapsing core-system, processor, servicing, claim, and ledger conflicts into a single metric without a reconciliation note.
Normalizing suspicious or consumer-impacting activity as ordinary variance instead of escalating fraud, AML, or sanctions risk.
Crossing from descriptive analytics into legal interpretation, claim adjudication, underwriting, accounting signoff, or policy override authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific insurance claim and prompt-payment rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline uses NAIC model-law materials for insurance claims, but state adoption and carrier-specific claims rules still require tenant jurisdiction and policy context at runtime.

Public sources constrain workflow and control posture, but tenant accounting policy, chart-of-accounts mapping, and disclosure-approval chains must be supplied before final financial treatment or disclosure language is inferred.

notes: Authoritative account, policy, claim, payment, dispute, and reconciliation truth comes from tenant-designated operational systems, payment systems, claims systems, servicing systems, and ledgers constrained by public regulatory baselines.

The lane stops before formal accounting treatment, legal interpretation, underwriting, fraud disposition, claims adjudication, or final customer-impact disclosure signoff even when analysis is operationally urgent.
